headline: PENDING — rigor protocol pre-registered; no >=3-run answer!=judge live run has been performed (key-gated)
criticalFailures: not_run: no --relay run with --runs>=3 and --answer-spec != --judge-spec has been executed (requires OpenRouter/LLMHub keys via the Actions secret store)
open_world_refs_not_measured: the --retrieve arm (Wikipedia truth-refs) that would MEASURE ref-independence has not been run
note: Intentionally PENDING. The harness already supports the full rigor protocol (--runs N + bootstrap CI, --answer-spec/--judge-spec enforced-to-differ, --retrieve open-world), and the multi-run CI aggregation is exercised offline in CI via --fake (tests/test_source_contamination_rigor.py). But --fake is plumbing, not evidence, and no live answer!=judge >=3-run result exists. Promotion to 'validated' follows the pre-registered verdicts in the measurement_spec: >=3 runs/family, answer!=judge, and an open-world --retrieve arm, with caught-rate CI-high and over-block CI-low on >=2 families. Until then the row stays CANDIDATE (where the 2026-06-28 single-run result sits). canClaimAGI stays false. See source-contamination-live-multifamily-2026-06-28 in agi-proof/failure-ledger.md.
